metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC #4, e.g.: `make changelog VERSION=v0.0.0-test && git diff --exit-code CHANGELOG.md` (second invocation exits 0, meaning no further change).
findings.finding: Acceptance criterion #4 (idempotence) states a behavioral claim — 'produces no second diff' — but supplies no proving command, leaving an executor with no concrete verification step. This violates E6 check (a): every completion-relevant claim needs a concrete proving command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 4: 'Idempotence of the sanctioned path: running `make changelog VERSION=<current>` twice with no new commits produces no second diff.'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #4: 'prove: `make changelog VERSION=vX.Y.Z` (first run), then run `make changelog VERSION=vX.Y.Z` again; verify with `git diff --exit-code CHANGELOG.md` — exit code 0 means idempotent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a guard in the Makefile `changelog` target (or document it in the AC) that checks whether `## [$(VERSION)]` already appears in CHANGELOG.md and exits cleanly (no-op) if so, making pre-tag re-invocations truly idempotent.
findings.finding: The idempotence AC ('running make changelog VERSION=<current> twice with no new commits produces no second diff') is mechanically broken in the pre-tag window. The invocation `git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --prepend CHANGELOG.md` uses `--unreleased`, which selects commits not yet covered by an *existing* git tag. Because the tag is only created after the generate→curate→commit→tag sequence, a maintainer running the command twice before tagging will get the same unreleased commits prepended a second time, producing a duplicate section and failing the idempotence property the plan claims.
findings.location: What section — invocation interface; AC — 'Idempotence of the sanctioned path'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Makefile target, before invoking git-cliff, add: `if grep -q '^## \[$(VERSION)\]' CHANGELOG.md; then echo 'Changelog section for $(VERSION) already present — nothing to do.'; exit 0; fi`. Update the AC proof command accordingly: it must pass on a second run without producing a diff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --prepend` is actually idempotent when the tag hasn't been applied yet, and if not, add an explicit guard (e.g. check whether a `## [VERSION]` header already exists in CHANGELOG.md before running, and error/skip if it does) — or adjust the AC to reflect the actual behaviour.
findings.finding: The plan's idempotence AC asserts that `make changelog VERSION=<current>` run twice with no new commits 'produces no second diff', but git-cliff's `--prepend` mode does not deduplicate or detect already-prepended sections. Since VERSION is passed as a future tag (not yet applied to the repo), the commits remain 'unreleased' on both invocations, so a second run would prepend a duplicate section — violating the AC. This is an unverified capability assertion about git-cliff's `--prepend` behaviour.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — idempotence AC ('running `make changelog VERSION=<current>` twice with no new commits produces no second diff')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Makefile guard before the git-cliff invocation: `grep -q "^## \[$(VERSION)\]" CHANGELOG.md && { echo 'ERROR: $(VERSION) already in CHANGELOG.md — delete the section before re-running'; exit 1; }`. Document this as the second-run contract in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `grep -c` cardinality check with a shell assertion, e.g.: `[ "$(grep -c '^## \[' CHANGELOG.md)" -ge 8 ]` so the command itself exits non-zero when the threshold is not met.
findings.finding: Acceptance criterion #1's cardinality check (`grep -c "^## \[" CHANGELOG.md` ≥ 8) is not a pass/fail shell command — `grep -c` prints a number; the '≥ 8' comparison lives only in prose. An executor running the literal command cannot determine pass/fail without a surrounding assertion. This is verify-command defect type #5 (missing cardinality assertion).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that proves the version-mismatch guard logic exists in the Makefile, e.g. `grep -qE 'git-cliff.*--version|GIT_CLIFF_VERSION.*version' Makefile` (or equivalent), so the 'errors if version mismatches' requirement is verifiably met.
findings.finding: The 'What' section specifies that the Makefile target must 'error clearly if git-cliff is missing or its --version mismatches the pin', but no AC proves the version-mismatch guard logic is present — AC #3 only checks `grep -q 'GIT_CLIFF_VERSION' Makefile`, which confirms the variable exists but not that a guard comparing `git-cliff --version` output to the pin is wired up.
findings.location: AC #3 (Makefile interface) and 'What' section, Makefile bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC #3 with: `grep -qE '(git-cliff.*--version|version.*GIT_CLIFF_VERSION)' Makefile` to confirm the guard compares the installed binary's version against the pinned value.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or augment AC #4 with a concrete, shell-reproducible proof, e.g. `make changelog VERSION=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0) && git diff --exit-code CHANGELOG.md` (after an initial run with no new commits), consistent with the grep-formula style of the other ACs.
findings.finding: AC #4 (idempotence) asserts a behavioral property — 'running `make changelog VERSION=<current>` twice with no new commits produces no second diff' — but, unlike every other AC in the plan, provides no grep formula or concrete proof mechanism, leaving it unverifiable in the same reproducible way as the other ACs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, bullet #4 (Idempotence of the sanctioned path)
findings.suggested_fix: AC #4: `make changelog VERSION=<last-tag> && git diff --exit-code CHANGELOG.md` — exits 0 (no diff) when no unreleased commits exist, directly proving idempotence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `<exact 2.x pin chosen at execution>` with a concrete version string (e.g. `2.6.1`) or state 'pin to the current stable 2.x release at time of plan authoring and record it here' so the executor has a deterministic default.
findings.finding: The Makefile section specifies `GIT_CLIFF_VERSION := <exact 2.x pin chosen at execution>` — an explicit placeholder that defers the version-pin decision to the executing agent. This forces the agent to 'choose an appropriate version' rather than implementing a stated default, which is precisely the placeholder pattern E2 flags.
findings.location: What section — Makefile bullet: 'GIT_CLIFF_VERSION := <exact 2.x pin chosen at execution>'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note such as: 'At time of writing, the current stable release is 2.6.1; use that unless a newer 2.x patch is available, and record the chosen value in this plan before implementation.' Then update both the Makefile bullet and the docs/releasing.md install spec to show the literal pinned version.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a guard in the `make changelog` target (or document that git-cliff natively suppresses empty output) so that invoking the target with no new unreleased commits produces zero diff, satisfying the idempotence AC.
findings.finding: The idempotence AC requires that running `make changelog VERSION=<current>` twice with no new commits produces no second diff, but the invocation spec (`git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --prepend CHANGELOG.md`) does not address git-cliff's behavior when `--unreleased` finds zero commits — git-cliff may still prepend an empty version section, violating idempotence. The Makefile target design (What section) and the idempotence AC are therefore potentially contradictory.
findings.location: AC: 'Idempotence of the sanctioned path' bullet vs. What section: invocation `git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --prepend CHANGELOG.md`
findings.suggested_fix: In the Makefile target, capture git-cliff output first (`git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --output /tmp/cliff_preview.md`) and abort (or skip prepend) if the output is empty or contains only a header line, before running the `--prepend` form.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC bullet 1) that explicitly verifies the initial run covered all published releases, e.g., `grep -q "\[v0.1.0\]" CHANGELOG.md` to confirm history reaches the first release.
findings.finding: The described one-time initial run ("back through v0.1.0; ledger quality accepted for history; executed manually") is a distinct deliverable with its own procedural steps, but no AC bullet directly verifies the initial-run procedure was performed correctly or that the resulting history is navigable. The `≥8 sections` check in the CHANGELOG.md AC only indirectly implies history was populated.
findings.location: What section: 'the initial run (once, back through v0.1.0; ledger quality accepted for history) is executed manually and documented as one-time'; AC bullet 1: `grep -c "^## \[" CHANGELOG.md` ≥ 8
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC bullet 1: `&& grep -q '\[v0.1.0\]' CHANGELOG.md` (or the actual first tag name) to prove back-history was populated to the beginning.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a guard in `make changelog` (or document git-cliff's native behavior) for the zero-unreleased-commits case: either abort with a clear message or ensure no empty section is prepended.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what happens when `git cliff --unreleased` finds zero unreleased commits: git-cliff may prepend an empty or skeletal version section (e.g., a header with no entries), silently dirtying CHANGELOG.md with a useless entry.
findings.location: What section / Makefile changelog target / AC: Makefile interface

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the edit-set (or explicitly acknowledge it as out-of-scope with a rationale) — its description of `release-notes.md` at line 55 needs a companion reference to `CHANGELOG.md` once the pointer line is added to `release-notes.md`.
findings.finding: docs/README.md:55 is an unenumerated consumer of the docs/release-notes.md change. It currently describes release-notes.md as 'agent-visible contract changes, newest first' with no mention of CHANGELOG.md. When the plan adds a pointer line at the top of release-notes.md (redirecting readers to CHANGELOG.md as the user-facing changelog), docs/README.md's description becomes stale — readers of the docs index will not know CHANGELOG.md exists or that release-notes.md's role has narrowed. The plan's Scope section lists this file as neither IN nor OUT, and does not acknowledge it as a known consumer.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line update to docs/README.md:55 in the Scope IN list, changing the description to reference both files, e.g.: '**[release-notes.md](release-notes.md)** — agent-visible contract changes (see also [CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md) for the user-facing changelog).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence rollback note in docs/releasing.md (or the plan's Scope section) stating that to revert the URL repoint, restore pyproject.toml line 78 to `docs/release-notes.md` and remove the pointer line — making the reversibility mechanism explicit.
findings.finding: The `[project.urls] Changelog` repoint (pyproject.toml:78) from `docs/release-notes.md` to `CHANGELOG.md` is explicitly acknowledged as a 'required edit' and paired with a pointer line in release-notes.md, satisfying acknowledgement. However, the plan provides no explicit rollback path or back-out instruction if the new CHANGELOG.md is found to be insufficient after publishing to PyPI — T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement.
findings.location: What section: 'Repoint [project.urls] Changelog (required edit; currently docs/release-notes.md, pyproject.toml:78)'; AC: `grep "^Changelog" pyproject.toml | grep -q "CHANGELOG.md"`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to docs/releasing.md: 'To roll back: revert pyproject.toml `[project.urls] Changelog` to `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/blob/main/docs/release-notes.md` and remove the cross-link pointer from release-notes.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step (or AC grep) to audit all in-repo docs for references to `docs/release-notes.md` used as the user-facing changelog, and update or redirect any found (e.g., README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md).
findings.finding: The plan repoints the pyproject.toml Changelog URL and adds a pointer in docs/release-notes.md, but does not include a step to audit other in-repo documents (e.g., README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or any other docs) that may currently reference docs/release-notes.md as the user-facing changelog — those references would become stale/misleading after this change.
findings.location: What section — 'Repoint [project.urls] Changelog' and 'Pointer line at the top of docs/release-notes.md'; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope/AC: `grep -r 'release-notes.md' docs/ README.md CONTRIBUTING.md | grep -v 'agent-visible\|contract changes'` must return no hits that present release-notes.md as the user changelog; add a remediation step for any hits found.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the `make changelog` target writes to a temp file first and moves it into place (or performs a git-level backup), so an interrupted write leaves CHANGELOG.md intact and recoverable.
findings.finding: The `--prepend` write to CHANGELOG.md is not atomic: if git-cliff is interrupted mid-write (crash, signal, disk-full), the file is left in a partially-written, corrupt state with no recovery path specified.
findings.location: What section / Makefile changelog target / AC: Idempotence of the sanctioned path
findings.suggested_fix: In the Makefile target: `git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) > /tmp/cliff_prepend.md && cat /tmp/cliff_prepend.md CHANGELOG.md > /tmp/changelog_new.md && mv /tmp/changelog_new.md CHANGELOG.md` (or rely on `--prepend` only if git-cliff itself guarantees atomic write — if so, cite that guarantee in the plan).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `--unreleased`, `--tag`, and `--prepend` against `git cliff --help` output for the specific 2.x version being pinned, and record the verified flag names in the plan (or in a comment in the Makefile target) to move evidence class from Partially-verified to Verified.
findings.finding: The plan asserts all three CLI flags — `--unreleased`, `--tag $(VERSION)`, and `--prepend CHANGELOG.md` — as the canonical invocation (`git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --prepend CHANGELOG.md`), but none of these flag names have been verified against `git cliff --help` output in this environment. They match training-data knowledge of git-cliff 2.x, but flag-level empiricism requires observation from `--help`, not memory. If any flag name changed between 1.x and 2.x (e.g. `--prepend` vs `--prepend-changelog`, or `--unreleased` vs `--unreleased-only`), the Makefile target will silently fail or error at runtime.
findings.location: What section — invocation spec: `git cliff --unreleased --tag $(VERSION) --prepend CHANGELOG.md`; AC #3 Makefile interface grep checks
findings.suggested_fix: Run `git cliff --help` for the chosen 2.x pin and confirm the exact flag spellings. For git-cliff 2.x these are expected to be `--unreleased`, `--tag <TAG>`, and `--prepend <FILE>` — but record the observed output in the plan. Also note that `--version` output format for the guard check should be observed (e.g. `git cliff 2.6.1` vs `git-cliff 2.6.1`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact PyPI package name for git-cliff at the chosen 2.x pin (run `pip index versions git-cliff` or check pypi.org/project/git-cliff) and record the confirmed package name + a fallback install path (e.g. `cargo install git-cliff` or a GitHub Releases binary) in docs/releasing.md.
findings.finding: The plan specifies installation via `pipx install git-cliff==<pin>`, treating git-cliff as a PyPI-installable package. git-cliff is primarily a Rust binary; its PyPI presence (as a Python wrapper that downloads/bundles the Rust binary) is real but partially-verified in this environment. If the PyPI package name differs from `git-cliff` (e.g. it is `gitcliff` or only available via a Rust toolchain), the documented install path in `docs/releasing.md` would silently fail for new maintainers.
findings.location: What section — 'Install path documented in docs/releasing.md as `pipx install git-cliff==<pin>` (standalone Rust binary; NOT a pyproject dev extra)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to docs/releasing.md: a confirmed `pip index versions git-cliff` result for the pinned version, plus a cargo/binary fallback: 'If pipx install fails: cargo install git-cliff --version <pin> OR download from https://github.com/orhun/git-cliff/releases/tag/v<pin>'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
